text:
Market today: Tone is risk-on - Tesla's blowout delivery number is lifting sentiment across growth and tech. That's your world. The real driver is the market repricing EV/consumer demand higher, which spills into broad tech momentum. Macro noise (Russia strikes, geopolitics) hasn't cracked equities yet, but it's a wildcard overhead.

Your holdings: CRWD is the urgent problem - down 72.55% and your scanner stop is listed at 590, which is way above current price at 197.47. That stop is almost certainly stale/wrong - you need to verify it immediately, because on margin a zombie stop protects nothing. SOXL is at 205.20 with your stop at 200 - it's close, barely $5 of cushion on a 3x leveraged ETF that can gap hard. SPAL is down 12.96% and also leveraged - small move against you hits that 21.38 stop fast.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds 200 (hard stop zone) and whether CRWD's scanner setup at 192-entry actually gets tested.

Bottom line: Fix the CRWD stop situation right now - everything else is secondary.
plays: CRWD stop is broken at 590 - price is 197.47. Verify your actual stop immediately; scanner shows support ~189.42, so consider that as your real line in the sand.
SOXL: You're at 205.20 with a 200 stop and down 19%. On a 3x ETF on margin, a gap through 200 compounds fast - confirm stop is live and tight.
No new buys today - scanner has CRWD and TECL setups but CRWD needs your stop fixed first, and adding leverage here while existing leveraged positions are stressed is not the move.
